exemplar.title: How successfully did Bitty Smith use characterization, themes, and symbolism to illustrate the socioeconomic and educational struggles of middle-class immigrant families in American societies?

detailedExemplarData.title: How successfully did Bitty Smith use characterization, themes, and symbolism to illustrate the socioeconomic and educational struggles of middle-class immigrant families in American societies?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: The investigationâs topic is clearly stated in the title and sustained through coherent sign-posting.
Research question is explicit and consistently referred back to throughout the discussion.
Methodology section outlines an interdisciplinary approach linking literary analysis and sociological data.
weaknesses: Minor author-name misspelling (âBettyâ vs âBitty Smithâ) and occasional grammatical slips reduce polish.
Research question scope is slightly over-broad in places (e.g., âmiddle-classâ vs âworking-classâ).
Methodology lacks detail on source selection criteria and breadth of academic scholarship.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student communicates the topic with clarity and purpose and maintains a focused line of inquiry. The research question, while a bit diffuse in its socioeconomic framing, is clearly linked to the body of work. The methodology demonstrates informed selection but stops short of the full range and rigour expected at the top mark. Minor lapses in naming and precision prevent a perfect 6, but overall the criterion is met very well at level 5.

strengths: Displays good knowledge of the novelâs characters, themes, and historical immigration context.
Sources (Ngai, Borjas, National Academies) are generally well-chosen and used correctly.
Subject-specific terms (symbolism, social mobility, intergenerational poverty) are deployed accurately.
weaknesses: Engagement with literary criticism is sparse; quotations are under-used and sometimes lack page references.
Occasional phrasing problems and inconsistent citation abbreviations.
Opening generalizations about immigration could be more narrowly analytical.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows a coherent understanding of both the literary text and relevant sociological sources, using terminology competently. However, deeper engagement with secondary literary scholarship and more consistent citation practices are needed for the very top level. This performance aligns squarely with a mark of 4.

strengths: Research sources are generally relevant to the question and thoughtfully connected to the novelâs themes.
Analysis moves beyond summary, comparing fictional portrayal with sociological data.
Discussion is sustained and mostly coherent, with a clear line of argument.
weaknesses: Some assertions lack specific supporting statistics or citations.
Counter-arguments and critical evaluation are sometimes rhetorical rather than fully developed.
Repetitive phrasing and occasional drift in the conclusion weaken impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student consistently presents a âgoodâ level of analysis and argument development, supported by relevant research. Yet recurrent lapsesâincomplete evidential support, under-developed counterpoints and occasional repetitionâhold the work at the entry point of the 4â6 band. A mark of 4 reflects this solid but not yet fully mature critical thinking.

strengths: Comprehensive structure includes title page, TOC, methodology, discussion, conclusion and references.
Logical use of headings (e.g., âFrancie Nolan,â âThe American Dream: Reality or Myth?â) aids navigation.
Pages are numbered and major sections are clearly labeled.
weaknesses: Citation style is inconsistent; line spacing and coding artefacts (e.g., â{5}ââââ, â$20<sup>th</sup>$â) detract from layout.
Parenthetical references often lack page numbers; footnotes are absent.
Table of contents could benefit from leader dots for professional polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report meets all organisational requirements and is easy to follow, but persistent formatting inconsistencies and artefacts limit its presentation quality. These issues place the work at the lower end of the 1â2 band, warranting a score of 2.

strandSelections.reasoning: A short âMethodologyâ section explains an interdisciplinary approach that mixes close reading with sociological sources.  The student cites half-a-dozen secondary texts and occasionally integrates statistics, showing some informed selection.  Yet the range is limited (one core sociology report is repeatedly recycled, literary scholarship on Smith is absent) and citation style is inconsistent.  Overall the methodology is âmostly completeâ but lacks the breadth and rigour characteristic of the top band, so a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

Basic formatting elements (title, word count, headings, TOC) are provided, but citation style is inconsistent, line spacing fluctuates, and there are visible coding artefacts (e.g., â{5}ââââ, â$20<sup>th</sup>$â).  Parenthetical references sometimes lack page numbers, and footnotes are absent.  Layout therefore shows several minor lapses, aligning with âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectly applied,â meriting 2 in the 1-2 band.

The student locates and employs several relevant secondary sources and connects them to the novel; the research chosen (immigration policy, education inequality) is generally pertinent to the question.  While the bibliography is not extensive, and some claims rely on a single report, most evidence remains clearly relevant, warranting a mark in the upper half of the 4-6 band.

The studentâs topic â the effectiveness of Betty Smithâs portrayal of immigrant struggle in A Tree Grows in Brooklyn â is clearly announced in the title and re-stated in the opening paragraph.  Headings and repeated sign-posting keep the focus on symbolism, characterization and theme throughout, giving the essay a recognisable purpose.  Minor lapses (misspelling âBetty,â occasional grammatical confusion) slightly blur precision, so the work falls short of the absolute polish expected for the top of the band, but overall the reader can at all times identify what is being investigated and why.  This aligns best with the 5-6 descriptor.

The discussion is sustained and largely coherent; the student continually interrogates whether Smith âromanticises toughness,â weighing literary choices against sociological realities.  There is some critical evaluation, though counter-arguments are sometimes posed rhetorically rather than fully developed, and conclusions occasionally drift into repetition.  This meets the descriptor of âgood argument development with partially critical evaluation,â justifying a 5 in the 4-6 range.

The essay moves beyond plot summary to compare fictional representation with sociological data, posing evaluative questions about idealisation and structural barriers.  Assertions are usually supported with textual examples or statistics, though some points remain generalized and a few quotations are paraphrased without page reference.  Depth is therefore âgoodâ rather than âexcellent,â fitting comfortably in the 4-6 band at mark 5.

The essay follows EE conventions: title page, table of contents, introduction, methodology, body sections, reflection, conclusion and works cited.  Headings are logical and pagination is indicated.  While some headings are duplicated or awkwardly placed, the overall structure is clearly appropriate and all required components are present, fitting the top band for Presentation.

The essay demonstrates good knowledge of ATGIB, summarising characters, key scenes and symbols accurately, and showing awareness of historical immigration policy.  Sources such as Ngai, Borjas, and the National Academies report are relevant and generally used correctly, although quotation is sparse and engagement with literary criticism is weak.  The understanding is coherent but not consistently deep, fitting a 3-4 band with the higher mark justified by generally accurate content.

Subject terminology (e.g., symbolism, immigrant paradox, social mobility, inter-generational poverty) is used frequently and mostly accurately.  Occasional phrasing problems (âworld class studentsâ) and inconsistent citation abbreviations show lapses, but overall the student handles key literary and sociological concepts with competence.  This aligns with âmostly accurateâ usage, earning 4 in the 3-4 band.

The research question is explicitly stated and directly connected to the body of discussion; however, it is somewhat diffuse â it names three literary devices, claims to examine âmiddle-classâ families (the novel depicts working class), and uses the plural âAmerican societies,â which broadens the scope beyond what one novel can sustain.  Nevertheless, it remains sufficiently focused to guide analysis and is consistently referred back to.  These qualities correspond to a solid placement in the 3-4 band, meriting the upper mark within that range.

annotations.comment: The title effectively communicates the investigationâs scopeâsymbolism, social mobility, and immigrant struggleâoffering clear focus and purpose.

The research question repeats âBitty Smithâ and mislabels immigrant socioeconomic status; ensure correct author name and align scope (âworking-classâ vs âmiddle-classâ) with novel content.
The precise 4000-word count meets IB formatting requirements. Ensure consistency throughout submission.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly formatted, aiding navigation between sections.
The entry âJohnny Nolan.â includes an extraneous period within the TOC. Standardize punctuation to maintain consistency.
Consider adding leader dots between section titles and page numbers in the TOC to improve readability and professional presentation.
The introduction heading is appropriately labeled but would benefit from an explicit thesis statement that directly addresses the research question.
Opening paragraph makes broad generalizations about immigration. Narrow the focus and integrate a more analytical angle from the outset.
The inline markup for â20<sup>th</sup> centuryâ is inconsistent. Use LaTeX: $$20^{\text{th}}\text{ century}$$ for clarity and IB formatting compliance.
The methodology section is clearly indicated, establishing the interdisciplinary approach for literary and sociological analysis.
The methodology description lacks detail on source selection criteria and a broader range of academic scholarship, limiting rigor.
The subheading âFrancie Nolanâ clearly organizes character analysis, guiding the reader through thematic exploration.
Citation â(National Academies 120)â lacks page reference formatting consistency; include full bibliographic details or use parenthetical style correctly.
The character analysis occasionally summarizes plot rather than evaluating its significance; focus on interpreting how Francieâs education critiques systemic barriers.
The âKatie Nolanâ heading efficiently distinguishes her section, reinforcing structural clarity.
The standalone question âDoes Smith depict resilienceâ¦â disrupts flow. Integrate it into the argument to strengthen critical continuity.
Balanced coverage of Johnny Nolanâs character shows comprehensive character analysis across family members.
The âConnection to Dataâ section effectively demonstrates interdisciplinary synthesis, linking novel analysis to sociological findings.
The claim about immigrant menâs discrimination lacks a citation; include the specific source and page number for verification.
The thematic heading clearly frames discussion of socioeconomic struggles and the American Dream.
Repetitive phrasing in the conclusion of this section weakens impact; consolidate ideas and vary language to enhance clarity.
The subheading âThe American Dream: Reality or Myth?â effectively guides critical comparison of ideology versus evidence.
The assertion about intergenerational wage differentials is stated without supporting data; integrate specific statistics and citation.
The âSynthesis of Themesâ section skillfully brings together characterization, symbolism, and data analysis for a cohesive conclusion.
The reflection briefly describes challenges but stops short of evaluating planning decisions or next steps; deepen evaluation of research planning.
